summary: Iteration 10 verified the new login hardening (brute-force lockout + new-device email) end-to-end against the preview URL. BACKEND (6/6 pytest pass, /app/backend/tests/test_login_security.py, ~15s): (a) Brute-force on a throwaway email bf_test_<rand>@example.com — 5 wrong attempts each return 401 'Invalid email or password'; 6th returns 429 detail 'Too many failed attempts. Please try again in about N minute(s).' (N matches /\d+ minute/); even sending the REAL password while in the locked window still returns 429; mongo db.login_attempts contains >=5 docs for that email. (b) Isolation — while one throwaway is locked, hajraanwar157@gmail.com still logs in 200 + token (lockout is per-email, not global). (c) Clearing — 4 wrong attempts as hajra (below the 5-fail threshold so she's never locked) persist 4 docs in login_attempts; the very next correct login returns 200 AND login_attempts for that email becomes 0, proving _check_login_lock + delete_many on success works. (d) New-device email — login with a brand-new User-Agent 'QA-NewDevice-<rand>' produces the backend log line 'Email sent to hajraanwar157@gmail.com: New sign-in to your CAVI account' in /var/log/supervisor/backend.err.log within ~6s; a second login with the SAME User-Agent does NOT emit that log line (device key now in user.knownDevices). (e) Privacy — POST /auth/login response.user and GET /auth/me response.user both omit 'knownDevices' (public_user pops it) but both include a non-empty 'lastLoginAt' ISO string; raw mongo doc still has the hashed device key persisted (verified via direct pymongo read). (f) Regression — same Bearer token hits GET /api/wallets and GET /api/roi with HTTP 200. FRONTEND (Playwright on preview URL /login): wrong password (DefinitelyWrong!1) surfaces a sonner toast exactly reading 'Invalid email or password' (captured from [data-sonner-toast]); correct credentials (hajraanwar157@gmail.com / NewCavi@2026) trigger a 'Welcome back' toast and navigate to /app where the dashboard renders (Balance $525, Deposit Base $550, ROI/Wallets/Withdrawals/Settings sidebar — see .screenshots/iter10_post_login.png). Did NOT exercise repeated wrong attempts in the UI to avoid accidental lockout of the real user. FINAL STATE: db.login_attempts.deleteMany({email:'hajraanwar157@gmail.com'}) and deleteMany({email:/^bf_test_/}) run in teardown_module + a final mongosh sweep — both collections at 0; a final curl POST /api/auth/login as hajra returns HTTP 200, confirming the real user is NOT left locked.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py is now ~1330 lines — the auth concerns (login, lockout, device email, wallet-login, change-password) have grown into a clear router boundary; splitting into routers/auth.py + services/email_security.py is overdue.

_check_login_lock + login insert both touch db.login_attempts inside a single endpoint with no transaction. Under high concurrency a user could squeeze one extra attempt past the 5-cap (TOCTOU). At current scale fine; revisit with an atomic findOneAndUpdate counter if abuse appears.

Lockout is keyed only by email — there is no per-IP throttling. An attacker can permanently lock a victim's account by spamming 5 wrong passwords every 15 min from any IP (account-lock DoS). Consider: (a) cap by (email+IP) instead, or (b) on 429, also enforce a short captcha/cooldown, or (c) prefer ratelimiting + delayed response over hard-locking the legitimate owner.

_device_key uses sha256(User-Agent)[:16] — User-Agent is fully client-controlled and changes on every Chrome/Edge minor version, so legit users will get new-device emails on every browser auto-update. Consider hashing a coarser fingerprint (parsed UA family + OS family + a stable cookie/device-id) to reduce noise.

_maybe_notify_new_device silently swallows the 'first device ever' case — that's intentional for the migration of pre-feature users with empty knownDevices, but there's no marker on the user doc that 'silent seed has occurred'. If knownDevices is ever wiped (manual mongo edit, restore-from-backup) every existing device will be re-seeded silently. Consider a one-time 'devicesSeededAt' timestamp.

new-device email body embeds raw request user-agent (sliced to 120 chars) into HTML via f-string with no escaping. A crafted UA like '<script>...' won't execute in most mail clients but is still XSS-shaped content; pass it through html.escape() before injecting.

TTL index on login_attempts.ts is expireAfterSeconds=900 (15 min) — matches LOGIN_LOCK_MINUTES, but the constant and the index argument are duplicated literally. Use LOGIN_LOCK_MINUTES*60 in both places (already done in startup_db_client — good; just call this out as the single source of truth).
updated_files: /app/backend/tests/test_login_security.py (new pytest suite, 6 tests / 5 classes, ~15s, JUnit XML at /app/test_reports/pytest/iter10_login_security.xml)
test_credentials: hajraanwar157@gmail.com / NewCavi@2026 (final login curl = 200, login_attempts cleared). Superadmin: superadmin@cavi.io / Cavi@Admin2025 (not exercised this iteration).
seed_data_creation: Throwaway emails bf_test_<uuid>@example.com used only as keys in db.login_attempts (never inserted into users). All bf_test_* docs and any hajra failures deleted in teardown_module + a final mongosh sweep — verified counts == 0. New-device email test sent 1 live SMTP 'New sign-in' alert to hajraanwar157@gmail.com (expected per spec).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Reusable suite: /app/backend/tests/test_login_security.py — 6 tests, 5 classes, ~15s. Loads REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL from /app/frontend/.env and MONGO_URL/DB_NAME from /app/backend/.env via python-dotenv; talks straight to the preview URL + mongo. New-device email assertion tails /var/log/supervisor/backend.err.log for 'Email sent to <addr>: New sign-in to your CAVI account' (NOT backend.out.log — the logger writes to stderr). teardown_module ALWAYS clears db.login_attempts for hajra + any bf_test_* leftovers. If you need to reproduce a lock fast: 5x curl POST /api/auth/login with wrong password — TTL on ts auto-expires after 15 min, or manually deleteMany. UI testids on /login: login-email, login-password, login-password-toggle, login-submit, goto-forgot, goto-signup; toast container uses [data-sonner-toast].
rca of the issue: N/A — no bugs found. Brute-force lockout, success-clears-attempts, device-key dedupe, new-device SMTP, knownDevices privacy strip, and lastLoginAt exposure all match the spec. Only one test-script fix needed during the run: initial assertion was reading backend.out.log; logger.info routes to stderr so switched to backend.err.log.
